home.title: Miles Zeilstra | Creative Developer
home.description: Portfolio of Miles Zeilstra - Creative Developer specializing in React, TypeScript and interactive web experiences.

about.heading: About me
about.paragraph1: I'm Miles, a second-year CMGT student at Rotterdam University of Applied Sciences. What excites me about development is the creative freedom: coming up with an idea and bringing it to life myself.
about.paragraph2: From web applications to games in Godot: I'm always building, even outside of school. I learn best by making things.
about.paragraph3: Outside of my projects I'm always trying to improve. I read books, follow communities about development, design and AI, and apply that knowledge to how I work.

projects.heading: Projects
projects.description: A selection of my best work. Click a project for more details.

ux.title: User Experience
ux.description: I think from the user's perspective. How does something feel, what do you expect at this moment, where do you get stuck? Critically examining and improving that is what I'm good at.

frontend.title: Frontend
frontend.description: A good interface is something you feel. I build with attention to how things move, respond and feel. From layout to animation.

ideaToProduct.title: From concept to product
ideaToProduct.description: I start with the fundamentals: what problem does it solve and for whom? Wireframes, user flows and a clear concept before writing code. Then iterating with an eye for detail. I strive for perfection in every part.

fullstack.title: Fullstack
fullstack.description: I build complete web applications from frontend to backend. From React interfaces to Node.js APIs and databases. I feel just as at home in the frontend as in the backend.

contact.heading: Get in touch
contact.description: Interested in my work or looking for a motivated intern? Let me know.

form.name: Name
form.successDetail: I'll get back to you as soon as possible.
form.fallback: You can also reach me directly at

draftgap.description: A champion pool optimizer for League of Legends. Analyze matchups, find bad matchups and get data-driven suggestions.
draftgap.metaDescription: DraftGap - A League of Legends champion pool optimizer with matchup analysis and suggestions. Built by Miles Zeilstra.
draftgap.context: I built DraftGap to solve a common problem for League of Legends players: knowing which champions to pick against specific opponents. The app analyzes your champion pool against all matchups using real winrate data from U.GG, finds your bad matchups, and suggests champions that would strengthen your pool.
draftgap.role: Full development from concept to deployment. Responsible for the data pipeline, matchup analysis engine, and the complete React frontend. Focused on turning raw winrate data into actionable draft advice.
process: Scraped matchup data from U.GG for all roles and tiers
Built a matchup engine with bi-directional winrate lookups and gap detection
Designed a responsive dashboard with blind pick rankings, ban targets and pool grading
Added enemy lookup with champion profiles, counter picks and build recommendations
Implemented pool sharing via URL and a pool grade scoring system
Duo synergy analysis for bot/support roles
draftgap.result: A polished, data-driven tool that helps players make better draft decisions. Features real-time matchup analysis, responsive mobile design, and shareable pool links.

getcooked.description: A recipe platform where users can browse, add and search recipes. Built with Laravel.
getcooked.metaDescription: GetCooked - A Laravel recipe platform with search, filtering and user accounts. Built by Miles Zeilstra.
getcooked.context: I independently built a complete recipe platform as a school project. Users can upload private recipes or submit them for public review. Verified users can post publicly directly. An admin panel manages submissions and user permissions.
getcooked.role: Full development from concept to working product. This was my first major Laravel project, where I focused on setting up a complete MVC architecture.
process: ERD design and user stories
Role-based authentication with admin panel
Recipe status workflow (private, pending, approved, rejected)
Verified users system and spam prevention
Search and filter system with query scopes
Notification system for status updates
getcooked.result: A fully functional recipe application. Through this project I mastered Laravel and gained experience with MVC architecture.

natuurmoment.description: An interactive group game that guides players through nature areas with photo bingo and quiz questions.
natuurmoment.metaDescription: NatuurMoment - An interactive nature game with photo bingo and quiz questions. Laravel/Livewire team project by Miles Zeilstra.
natuurmoment.context: Together with a team I developed an interactive nature game. Players complete bingo challenges by taking photos and answer multiple-choice questions about the location, while competing for the highest score.
natuurmoment.role: Fullstack development with a focus on backend in a team of 5. Responsible for the style guide, wireframes and concept development. Built the complete admin panel, organizer dashboard and core features.
process: Concept development and style guide/wireframes design in Figma
Role-based system: player, organizer and admin
Quiz system with multiple-choice questions and score calculation
Organizer dashboard: create games, manage players, live monitoring
Admin panel with content management and statistics (ratings, age distribution, trends)
natuurmoment.result: A fully functional game that ran live with real users. The statistics dashboard showed engagement metrics such as average ratings per location and age distribution.

sonarpoppy.description: A music recommendation API that generates personalized track suggestions using a hybrid scoring algorithm.
sonarpoppy.metaDescription: SonarPoppy - A hybrid music recommendation engine with genre vectors, collaborative filtering and personalized scoring. Built by Miles Zeilstra.
sonarpoppy.context: In a team I built a music recommendation API that serves two apps (SonarPop & Poppy) with personalized suggestions. The system combines three pillars: genre vectors, collaborative filtering and user feedback.
sonarpoppy.role: Responsible for the entire recommendation algorithm: the hybrid scoring system, genre vector matching, collaborative filtering and the feedback loop. Also built the API architecture with extensive endpoints for the frontend: filters, sorting options, an onboarding flow and genre sliders for personalization. Wrote 70+ tests.
process: Designed a 20-dimensional genre vector system with cosine similarity scoring
Built collaborative filtering using Last.fm track and artist similarity data
Created a 5-position dial system that controls recommendation adventurousness (bubble filter)
Implemented dual authentication: API keys for apps, JWT tokens for users
User feedback loop: likes, dislikes and play history influence future recommendations
Integrated Spotify and Deezer APIs for track metadata and audio previews
Wrote 70+ unit and integration tests with mongodb-memory-server
sonarpoppy.result: A fully functional recommendation engine that learns from user behavior. The hybrid scoring algorithm combines genre similarity with collaborative filtering, producing personalized results that improve over time.

claude-config.description: A modular framework of 50+ skills and 20+ agents that automate development workflows through AI.
claude-config.metaDescription: Claude Config - An AI workflow automation framework with 50+ skills and 20+ specialized agents. Built by Miles Zeilstra.
claude-config.context: I built this framework over several months to streamline my own development workflow. The core consists of two pipelines: a dev pipeline (define → build → test) that takes features from requirements to working code, and a frontend pipeline with a custom visual inspect tool that lets you click elements in the browser and edit them directly through AI.
claude-config.role: Solo developer responsible for the full setup: how skills, agents and pipelines work together, the prompt engineering behind each skill, and all implementations including the visual tooling.
process: Built a dev pipeline: define (requirements gathering) → build (TDD or implementation-first per requirement) → test (hybrid verification with fix loops)
Developed a frontend iterate tool: a browser overlay that inspects elements (file location, classes, computed styles) and sends them via clipboard to AI for targeted edits with live HMR
Built an interactive backlog dashboard with kanban board, sorting/filtering and GitHub integration
Created 20+ specialized agents including OWASP security scanners and code researchers
Built 50+ reusable skills across domains: development, frontend, game dev, security
Cross-platform support for Linux VPS and Windows local development
claude-config.result: A framework I use daily across all my projects. The dev pipeline has taken dozens of features from requirements to production, and the frontend tool enables visual iteration without having to manually search for code. On top of that there are thinking skills for brainstorming, decision-making and analysis, debug skills, security scanners and more, together a complete toolkit for the entire development process.
